channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA picks first European astronaut for Artemis mission - DW.com

item.title: Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test - Space
item.description:
1. Thruster breakthrough? New 2-in-1 propulsion system is about to get an in-space test  Space
  2. New propulsion system could make tiny satellites both fast and fuel-efficient  MIT News
  3. MIT engineers just collapsed the oldest tradeoff in satellite propulsion — one tank, two thrusters, and a quiet rewrite of how cubesats are designed  Space Daily
  4. A “Green” Dual-Mode Engine is About to Give CubeSats the Best of Both Worlds  Universe Today
  5. MIT’s new spacecraft engine could send tiny satellites to Mars  ScienceDaily

item.title: A lack of sex held back life's diversity for millions of years, fossil study finds - Phys.org
item.description:
1. A lack of sex held back life's diversity for millions of years, fossil study finds  Phys.org

  5. Lack of sex may have slowed evolution for millions of years  Courthouse News

item.title: Astronomers found a galaxy in the throes of death – and they know what’s killing it - The Conversation
item.description:

item.title: Only 0.001 percent of the deep ocean floor has ever been seen by human eyes — an area roughly the size of Rhode Island — according to a May 2025 study in Science Advances, meaning more than 99.999 percent of the seafloor covering two-thirds of our pl - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Only 0.001 percent of the deep ocean floor has ever been seen by human eyes — an area roughly the size of Rhode Island — according to a May 2025 study in Science Advances, meaning more than 99.999 percent of the seafloor covering two-thirds of our pl  Space Daily
  2. Ping-pong sponges, ‘black smokers’ and floating somethings: the secrets of the deep sea  The Guardian
  3. 13 Creepiest Things You Can Find at the Bottom of the Ocean  Yahoo
  4. Deepest parts of Earth's oceans are a much more thriving habitat than scientists assumed  Earth.com

item.title: NASA’s Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS - NASA Science (.gov)
item.description:
1. NASA’s Webb Detects Methane on Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS  NASA Science (.gov)
  2. Radio scans find no alien tech from the latest interstellar comet  AP News
  3. Comet 3I/ATLAS has methane, unexpected discovery reveals  EarthSky
  4. 3I/ATLAS is only the third confirmed interstellar object ever detected passing through our solar system. It may be older than the Sun itself, and Webb observations show it is carrying an unusually carbon dioxide-rich coma as it swings past once before leaving fo  Space Daily
  5. SETI Institute Looks for Signs of Technology in Interstellar Visitor 3I/ATLAS  SETI
